title: rosa gallica f. leiophylla
intro: Rosa gallica f. leiophylla, also known as the French lavender rose or Rosa gallica var. leiophylla, is a subspecies of the Rosa gallica, an old garden rose with fragrant, pale pink flowers and dark green leaves. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and burns. The health benefits of Rosa gallica f. leiophylla include its ability to reduce inflammation, promote wound healing, and provide antioxidant protection. Rosa gallica f. leiophylla can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
